must_detect.matched_text:
INSIDE INFORMATION — Project ANVIL — Embargo until 15 July 2026 08:00 CET; Issuer: Nordivolt SE (ACM Exchange ticker: NVSE)
Board memo n°12/2026 records a cyber incident on 9 July 2026 affecting the Fiorenza DC; preliminary €3.2m revenue at risk (~3.1% of H2 guidance)
Attack vector: supplier SFTP compromise at Vantagebyte s.r.l. (processor); 2 1 , 7 9 0 HR files accessed, incl. limited Art. 9 data (biometric time‑clock hashes, trade‑union opt‑in flags)
Data subjects: employees in Member States Fiorenza and Helvonia; cross‑border transfer under SCCs; transfer impact assessment on file; DPO contact: privacy@nordivolt-se.eu
Identifiers observed: user pseudo‑ID E‑**‑9142, device‑id nvdl::a7‑9x‑Δ, shard ref “HR‑ARC/2026‑07”; no government IDs stored
Per MAR Art. 17(4), disclosure delay is justified due to ongoing containment and risk to a concurrent transaction (see Project ANVIL checklist §5)
Concurrent transaction: definitive SPA signed 5 July 2026 for acquisition of Oribaltics GmbH; consideration €42.5m (cash/earnout 70/30); locked‑box date 31 Dec 2025
Material adverse change: parties confirm the incident does not constitute a MAC under clause 12.4 of the draft SPA
IR draft (not for publication) forecasts operational EBITDA impact ≤0.6% FY26; CSRD double‑materiality note updated; PAI‑14 (data breaches) flagged; SFDR Art. 4 disclosures unchanged
NCA context: notification to Baltic Securities Authority within 72h prepared; ACM Exchange surveillance briefed; internal embargo ends per above unless extended
Public‑source reference: our 2025 sustainability report already disclosed the legacy firewall decommissioning timeline; the present incident is unrelated to that project
Contacts: Sir Alaric Bronte (Chair), Dr. Éva Marković (DPO), Ms. Pilar Costa (Head of IR); tel +32 000 000 000; backchannel anvil-brief@secure.nordivolt-se.eu
